acceptance_criteria_for_revision: artifact_contract precheck passes and workbook parsing is possible using provided text_export or visible formulas (verifier can programmatically read formulas or validate via the provided text file);
expert_memo.md is complete (no truncation) and contains the required per-row before/after table and mapping to cell ranges;
no inconsistency between memo numbers, workbook cells, and actual_outputs.json
extra_model_fields.follow_up: After submitting 290-revised, notify verifier to re-run automated checks and include any parser logs if parsing still fails so we can iterate quickly.
qa_checks_before_submission: open the revised CFD_surrogate_4.xlsx in a standard spreadsheet viewer and confirm that formulas are visible in-cell for the key calculations;
verify formula_cells.txt exactly matches the formulas present in the workbook Formulas sheet;
confirm expert_memo.md preview is not truncated (file size under preview limits) and includes the per-row calculation table;
run a local script (or request verifier) to parse the text-export and confirm numeric matches to actual_outputs.json

planned_changes: Add file [EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] (or .csv) listing the exact Excel formulas and the cell ranges used for: abs diff, abs percent error, per-option adjusted values (O1,O2,O3), and scenario cost sums. Example row: 'Sheet1!E2 = ABS(C2 - B2) ; Sheet1!F2 = E2 / B2 ; ...'.

Update CFD_surrogate_4.xlsx to include a dedicated worksheet 'Formulas' that contains plain-text copies of the formulas used (so that even if openpyxl is unavailable, the formulas are visible to human and some automated parsers).

Replace expert_memo.md with a complete, non-truncated version that includes a compact verification table showing for each record: inputs, abs diff (numerator), denominator, computed percent error, pass/fail for >15% rule, porosity support flag, and the recalculated percent error after each remediation option (with explicit adjusted numerators/denominators and scenario cost).

Add a short memo section that maps memo rows/tables to exact workbook cell ranges and states which artifact the verifier should parse first (e.g., 'See Sheet1 rows 2-4, columns B-F; see Formulas sheet rows 2-10 for textual formulas').

Recompute actual_outputs.json if any adjustments were needed and re-run the deterministic artifact-contract precheck; attach the resulting precheck output and ensure artifact previews are not truncated when uploaded.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Deliverables present and math in the expert memo is consistent with the input packet; deterministic artifact-contract and model grading passed. Primary weaknesses are machine-verifiability gaps: the .xlsx could not be parsed programmatically in this environment (openpyxl unavailable) and the memo preview delivered to the grader was truncated. These limitations mean automated verifiers relied on the memo preview and trace rather than direct, machine-validated workbook formulas. Recommended fixes are targeted and low-effort: include a full text export of key spreadsheet formula cells and a concise per-row before/after calculation table in the memo, and ensure the workbook includes visible formulas or a worksheet with plain-text formula cells so verifiers can validate automatically.
